codeReview.summary: Compared the original failed fix (83898ff) and this re-run fix (f864eba). The new implementation closes the specific snapshot-lag race by eagerly deleting dropdown ownership at dispatch time via `DefaultPolicyDropdownHandle.notifyPeerPolicyWrite`, wired through `PoliciesState` chip dispatch wrapper, while retaining snapshot pruning as a safety net. The added race-reproduction test covers the exact failure window from the prior review and now asserts no `unset` is dispatched for the user-authored cell.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ui-screen-worker still effectively forces procedural deviations for logic/race fixes that touch no directly exercisable UI path in demo scenarios.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:23,51,53 require agent-browser walkthroughs; handoff 2026-04-22T02-54-28-383Z__fix-m3-default-policy-no-clobber-race-eager-drop__f0770410-492f-4166-943b-4a1e8182325c.json:42-43,71-73 documents no interactive chip surface in demo and `followedProcedure=false` despite deterministic test coverage.

summary: PASS: The re-run fix adequately addresses the original blocking ownership-race failure by introducing eager ownership drop on override dispatch and verifying the pre-snapshot default-switch race with targeted tests; no new blocking or non-blocking code defects were found in scope.
